intro: Transitioning from Java to Swift shifts a developer from the cross-platform JVM ecosystem into Apple's highly optimized, native compiled systems language. Swift combines the structural safety of rigid compile-time data typing with a highly concise, modern syntax that eliminates legacy object boilerplate. This migration is standard when building high-performance, low-latency client applications across iOS, macOS, and companion Apple device platforms.

diffs.from: Nominal class structures backed by runtime type checks and generic type erasure operations.
diffs.to: Strictly static system featuring reified generics, type inference, algebraic enum data types, and value-type structures.

diffs.from: Verbose, class-centric structure requiring semicolons, explicit getters/setters, and boilerplate try-catch blocks.
diffs.to: Modern, lightweight, expressive layout utilizing type inference, closures, guard statements, and native property accessors.

diffs.from: Managed completely by asynchronous JVM generational garbage collectors tracing heap references in the background.
diffs.to: Deterministic resource management handled via compile-time Automatic Reference Counting (ARC), requiring explicit handling of weak links.

diffs.from: Thread pools, synchronizers, CompletableFuture frameworks, and lightweight virtual threading processes.
diffs.to: Modern structured concurrency model utilizing language-level async/await primitives, Tasks, and safety-isolated Actors.

diffs.from: A massive enterprise toolkit covering everything from cross-platform server systems to database integrations.
diffs.to: A highly focused core framework tightly integrated with foundational layout capabilities and device operating system frameworks.

examples.title: Value Type Structs vs Reference Classes
examples.description: Converting an instantiable, heap-allocated Java data entity into an immutable, thread-safe Swift stack value structure.

examples.title: Safe Optional Binding Operations
examples.description: Refactoring Java null checks or Optional containers into elegant, native Swift guard statement unwrap operations.

tips.title: Prefer Structs Over Classes
tips.body: Build data models using 'struct' instead of 'class' by default to leverage stack allocation and automatic thread-safe copying.

tips.title: Prevent ARC Memory Leaks
tips.body: Break reference cycles between long-lived object instances by marking parent-child reference properties with 'weak' modifiers.

tips.title: Embrace Protocol Programming
tips.body: Replace deeply nested class inheritance patterns with flexible, decoupled protocol composition and default trait extensions.

tips.title: Leverage Guard for Guardrails
tips.body: Use 'guard' statements early within your method bodies to quickly handle error conditions and keep main logic code clean.
